headline: Intel's TeraFab Win A Major Credibility Boost For Foundry Arm: Analyst
summary: Intel Corp., Tesla Inc., xAI, and SpaceX finalized a landmark TeraFab partnership on Tuesday, positioning Intel as a core technology and manufacturing partner to scale next-generation AI and semiconductor production. Intel Secures Foundry Validation And Expansion Intel strengthens its foundry credibility by supplying technology, IP, and manufacturing expertise to power TeraFab, Neil Shah, a Counterpoint Research analyst, said on Wednesday. The deal supports its strategy to run large-scale fabs,

headline: SpaceX Is Going Public. Why a Tesla Merger Could Be Musk’s Real Endgame.
summary: SpaceX’s initial public offering may be the largest IPO ever, but it might just be a prelude to the largest merger ever—a combination of  Tesla  with Elon Musk’s space venture.  On April 1, SpaceX reportedly filed confidentially for an IPO.  It’s likely to raise $75 billion, more than double the $29 billion  Saudi Aramco  raised in 2019, and three times the $25 billion  Alibaba Group Holding  raised in 2014.

content.title: SpaceX Is Going Public. Why a Tesla Merger Could Be Musk’s Real Endgame.
content.summary: SpaceX’s initial public offering may be the largest IPO ever, but it might just be a prelude to the largest merger ever—a combination of  Tesla  with Elon Musk’s space venture. It’s likely to raise $75 billion, more than double the $29 billion  Saudi Aramco  raised in 2019, and three times the $25 billion  Alibaba Group Holding  raised in 2014.

headline: Tesla’s Low Cost SUV Revival Tests Growth, Margins And Cash Generation
summary: Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) is pushing ahead with development of an all new, lower cost compact electric SUV aimed at the global mass market. The new model is expected to sit below the Model 3 and Model Y in size and price, targeting cost conscious buyers in regions such as China, Europe, and North America. This move reverses earlier signals that a low cost vehicle project had been put on hold and refocuses attention on Tesla’s core EV lineup. The initiative comes as global EV demand has cooled...

headline: Elon Musk Takes Swipe At Gavin Newsom, Says Boring Could Build LA-SF Tunnel At Less Than 5% Cost
summary: Tesla Inc. CEO Elon Musk has touted that his tunneling startup, The Boring Company, can build a tunnel from Los Angeles to San Francisco that can exceed high-speed rail. Technological Marvel On Wednesday, Musk took to the social media platform X to express his criticism of Governor Gavin Newsom‘s (D-CA) High Speed Rail project from Los Angeles to San Francisco. “The @BoringCompany could build a Hyperloop tunnel from downtown SF to downtown LA for <5% of this [$120 billion] cost,” Musk said, quot

headline: Tesla’s ‘Death Cross’ Flashed Again. Should Investors Just Tune It Out?
summary: U.S. gasoline prices climbed above $4 a gallon again, up 30% since late February as Middle East tensions disrupted oil routes. Electric vehicle demand has slowed, and broader markets show their own technical stress. Against that backdrop, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares slipped into familiar territory this week. The 50-day simple moving average crossed below the 200-day ... Tesla’s ‘Death Cross’ Flashed Again. Should Investors Just Tune It Out?

headline: Tesla Stock Set for 8-Week Losing Streak—What It Needs to Do to Head Higher Again
summary: Tesla  stock is mired in a near-eight-week-long losing streak, with investors concerned about stagnant car sales, falling earnings estimates, and rising capital spending.  “Tesla is closing in on 10 billion Full Self-Driving miles traveled,” noted Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco in a Friday report.  FSD is Tesla’s highest-level driver assistance product, costing $99 a month, that can do most of the driving for Tesla owners.

FSD is Tesla’s highest-level driver assistance product, costing $99 a month, that can do most of the driving for Tesla owners.

headline: What’s Next for Amazon Stock? Charts Say Buy.
summary: As both a consumer and technology bellwether, Amazon’s performance continues to offer a valuable window into broader trends across spending, innovation, and digital infrastructure.  CEO Andy Jassy brought positive news this week when he downplayed fears of an AI bubble and indicated plans to maintain spending on the technology.  If that holds through the close, it would be Amazon’s first double-digit weekly gain since January 2023.

content.title: What’s Next for Amazon Stock? Charts Say Buy.
